loop_goal: Inspect, review, implement, validate, fix, and revalidate the mission-first PM workflow slice before stable release promotion.

stages.findings_summary: The repo was inspected for the PM-first intake gap and the stable release surfaces that still depended on manual discovery reconstruction.
stages.exit_condition: The missing PM-first intake and release traceability gap are explicit before implementation begins.

stages.findings_summary: The release scope stays bounded to mission-first intake, downstream propagation, and coherent starter-trace rebasing without widening ProductOS claims beyond the PM superpower core.
stages.exit_condition: The release narrative stays bounded and does not imply broader autonomous PM coverage than the repo evidence supports.

stages.findings_summary: ProductOS now exposes mission-first intake, mission-derived discover fallback, downstream mission propagation, and starter lifecycle trace rebasing in the core runtime and CLI.
stages.exit_condition: The mission-first workflow exists concretely in the repo and updates canonical starter artifacts coherently.

stages.findings_summary: Full repo validation passed after the mission-first runtime and lifecycle changes, including the new CLI regressions for discover sync and trace rebase.
stages.exit_condition: Automated validation confirms the bounded release slice is healthy and the new mission-first paths do not regress prior behavior.

stages.findings_summary: Starter-only replacement guards were tightened so initialized workspaces rebase cleanly while mature reference lifecycle examples remain untouched.
stages.exit_condition: Mission-first rewiring only affects safe starter traces and does not silently rewrite mature release evidence.

stages.findings_summary: Manual review confirms the mission-first PM workflow is releaseable as the next bounded ProductOS improvement slice.
stages.exit_condition: One explicit proceed decision exists and the new mission-first slice is acceptable as the stable line.

manual_review_summary: ProductOS V7.2.0 is acceptable because the PM now has one canonical mission intake that propagates through discover, downstream artifacts, and safe starter lifecycle trace rebasing.
next_action: Promote ProductOS to V7.2.0 stable and keep future release automation bounded by explicit Ralph evidence instead of ad hoc git actions.
